sn17.8:1.2: “Monks, gains, offerings, & fame are a cruel thing, a harsh, bitter obstacle to the attainment of the unexcelled rest from bondage.
sn17.8:1.3: “Have you heard the old jackal howling in the last hours of the night?”
sn17.8:1.5: “That old jackal is suffering from mange. He finds no pleasure whether he goes to a den, to the foot of a tree, or to the open air.
sn17.8:1.6: Wherever he goes, wherever he stands, wherever he sits, wherever he lies down, he is sunk in misery.
sn17.8:1.7: “In the same way there is the case where a certain monk is conquered by gains, offerings, & fame, his mind consumed. He finds no pleasure whether he goes to an empty dwelling, to the foot of a tree, or to the open air.
sn17.8:1.8: Wherever he goes, wherever he stands, wherever he sits, wherever he lies down, he is sunk in misery.
sn17.8:1.9: That’s how cruel gains, offerings, & fame are: a harsh, bitter obstacle to the attainment of the unexcelled rest from bondage. “Thus you should train yourselves: ‘We will put aside any gains, offerings, & fame that have arisen; and we will not let any gains, offerings, & fame that have arisen keep our minds consumed.’
sn17.8:1.10: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
